channel.title: The Sentinel Review — National Security Feed
channel.description: Automated national security news aggregation. Updated every 8 hours.

item.title: 🌍 Early Edition: June 2, 2026 | Just Security
item.description: Signup to receive the Early Edition in your inbox here. A curated weekday guide to major news and developments over the last 24 hours. Here’s today’s news: IRAN WAR – LEBANON OPERATIONS Iranian state media reported yesterday that Tehran was halting peace negotiations with the…

item.title: 🌍 Israel Has a Growing Anti-Christian Problem | Foreign Policy
item.description: A recent attack in Jerusalem highlights a worrying trend intensified by wartime nationalism.

item.title: 📍 The World’s Mineral Powers Seize Their Moment | Foreign Policy
item.description: Resource-rich countries haven’t always benefited from extraction. Can this time be different?

item.title: 🏴 US may consider placing nukes in Poland, Baltic States, report says | Defense News
item.description: While the discussions were ongoing, an agreement to expand U.S. nuclear hosting was not imminent, an unnamed source told the FT.

item.title: 🏴 NATO in Hormuz is not mission creep | Defense News
item.description: "For much of the last three decades, NATO has operated on the assumption that allied security cannot be defined solely by geography."

item.title: 📍 There Are Only Four Great Powers | Foreign Policy
item.description: An era of great-power competition has started—but not all would-be competitors qualify.

item.title: 🌏 Between Beijing and the Budget: The Domestic Realities of Taiwan’s Defense Spending Drama | War on the Rocks
item.description: On May 8, Taiwan’s Legislative Yuan broke a grueling six-month stalemate by passing a landmark $25 billion defense budget, catching many observers off guard. The vote brought sudden end to an agonizing legislative deadlock that had pushed U.S.-Taiwanese relations to the edge.…

item.title: 🌍 The Toll Booth at the Throat of World Trade | War on the Rocks
item.description: In late February 2026, Iran closed the Strait of Hormuz to foreign shipping. What began as a chaotic wartime closure has, in the past few days, hardened into something more consequential: an official sovereign toll regime, codified in Iranian law, and priced in cryptocurrency.On…

item.title: 📍 AUKUS Pillar Two lunges for an operational capability – underwater | ASPI Strategist
item.description: With the ‘signature project’ on payloads for uncrewed undersea vehicles (UUVs) announced by Australia, Britain and the United States on 30 May at the Shangri-La Dialogue, AUKUS Pillar Two transitions from scientific potential to realising ...

item.title: 📍 Desert e-bike race ‘the perfect’ place to test military-vehicle AI | Defense One
item.description: GDIT, AWS to outfit Baja 1000 race team with predictive-logistics gear.

item.title: 📍 2026 Has Been a Wild Ride for Southeast Asian Stock Markets | The Diplomat
item.description: Transparency concerns and the turmoil in global energy markets have created an unusual volatility in the region's capital markets.

item.title: 🏴 Thanks largely to robots, Ukraine is now talking about winning, not just surviving | Defense One
item.description: Uncrewed and autonomous systems—and the willingness to adapt to them—have neutered Russian advantages.

item.title: 🕵️ Preparing Australia for a future of overlapping risk | ASPI Strategist
item.description: Governments still organise around discrete shocks, yet the operating environment increasingly delivers continuous, concurrent and cascading pressures across economic, social, technological and environmental systems. Australia needs to shift from managing shocks to managing…

item.title: 🌍 Iran Embraces a Forever War | Foreign Affairs
item.description: Tehran’s new strategic calculus.

item.title: 🌏 The Next Challenge Facing the US-Philippine Alliance, and How They Plan to Overcome It | The Diplomat
item.description: This year's Balikatan exercise oriented itself more closely than ever to the Taiwan Strait -- and the possibility of a future conflict over the island.

item.title: 🌏 Sorry, Mr Carney. At Shangri-la, Indo-Pacific countries backed the rules-based order | ASPI Strategist
item.description: International rules still matter. That was the common theme at this year’s Shangri-La Dialogue in Singapore, particularly for Indo-Pacific nations. Australia’s deputy prime minister and defence minister, Richard Marles, told the conference, ‘The international rules-based ...

item.title: 📍 Indian PM Hosts Head of Myanmar’s Military Government in New Delhi | The Diplomat
item.description: New Delhi has defended its decision to engage with Min Aung Hlaing, who led the coup against Myanmar's democratically elected government in 2021.

item.title: 🏴 Philippine Senator Estrada Surrenders After Anti-graft Court Orders His Arrest | The Diplomat
item.description: Jose “Jinggoy” Estrada claims that the charges of plunder are a politically motivated outgrowth of the political feud between the Marcos and Duterte clans.

item.title: 🏴 Russian Trucks Get ‘Dazzle’ Paint To Throw Off AI-Enabled Drones | The War Zone
item.description: The disruptive paint appears designed to confuse machine-vision targeting systems as autonomous drones become a growing threat. The post Russian Trucks Get ‘Dazzle’ Paint To Throw Off AI-Enabled Drones appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 🌍 Is The U.S. Flying MQ-1 Predator Drones Again? | The War Zone
item.description: Questions surrounding the loss of an "MQ-1" to Iran highlight the possibilities and pitfalls of sending the Predator back into the fray. The post Is The U.S. Flying MQ-1 Predator Drones Again? appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 🏛️ Pentagon chief sounds ‘alarm’ over China’s buildup, urges allies to boost defense spending | Defense News
item.description: Hegseth urged Asian allies to ramp up military spending to counter China’s growing power.

item.title: 🌍 U.S.-Iran Ceasefire On The Brink After Tit-For-Tat Attacks (Updated) | The War Zone
item.description: Adding to the pressure, Iran has renewed threats to also close the Bab Al-Mandeb Strait, now an absolutely critical conduit for oil exports. The post U.S.-Iran Ceasefire On The Brink After Tit-For-Tat Attacks (Updated) appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 🌍 Trump Says Israel, Hezbollah to Halt Attacks as Iran Talks Continue | Foreign Policy
item.description: Tehran had said it would suspend negotiations over Israeli strikes in Lebanon.

item.title: 🌏 Suddenly, we hear of Australian–Japanese ‘strategic depth’. Here’s what it may mean | ASPI Strategist
item.description: A new element has just appeared in Australia–Japan defence and security cooperation: strategic depth. But the two countries, having mentioned it, haven’t gone far to explain what they mean by it. Nonetheless, some fairly safe ...

item.title: 🏛️ Ready, fire, aim: Pentagon cut workforce with little analysis before or since, GAO finds | Defense One
item.description: Defense officials concurred that lessons should be drawn—but gave no indication they will be.

item.title: 🏛️ The Navy wants next-generation munitions, so it’s spending millions on innovation hubs | Defense One
item.description: The service is teaming up with industrial park-builder ACMI to create an energetics facility in Maryland.

item.title: 📍 Experts react: Colombia’s presidential runoff pits the far right against the far left. What’s next? | Atlantic Council
item.description: Abelardo de la Espriella and Iván Cepeda, who represent opposite ends of the political spectrum, will move ahead to the June 21 runoff. The post Experts react: Colombia’s presidential runoff pits the far right against the far left. What’s next? appeared first on Atlantic…

item.title: 🌍 A Trump Deal With Iran Could Spell Trouble for Netanyahu | Foreign Policy
item.description: The Israeli leader needs a win ahead of a key election.

item.title: 🏛️ Littoral Combat Force Takes Up Station In Caribbean Under Navy’s New Deployment Concept | The War Zone
item.description: Here's where all of America's aircraft carriers and big-deck amphibious assault ships are as of June 1, 2026. The post Littoral Combat Force Takes Up Station In Caribbean Under Navy’s New Deployment Concept appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 🌍 Eurosatory ban on Israeli officials may hurt smaller firms, but majors still going to show | Breaking Defense
item.description: Both Elbit Systems and Rafael plan to attend Eurosatory, despite a new ban on Israeli government officials attending.
